WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT user_id, purchase_date,
        (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) * 12 
         + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD'))) AS month_num
    FROM purchase
),
streaks AS (
    SELECT user_id,
        purchase_date,
        month_num,
        month_num - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_num) AS grp
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    SELECT user_id, grp, COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
longest_streaks AS (
    SELECT user_id, MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT longest_streak, COUNT(*) AS number_of_users
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY longest_streak DESC

SELECT DISTINCT o.user_id, o.email
FROM orders o
WHERE 
  -- Condition 1: luxury + New York
  (o.segment = 'luxury' AND o.destination = 'New York')
  OR
  -- Condition 2: luxury + London
  (o.segment = 'luxury' AND o.destination = 'London')
  OR
  -- Condition 3: basic + New York with total revenue > 2000
  o.user_id IN (
    SELECT user_id
    FROM orders
    WHERE segment = 'basic' AND destination = 'New York'
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING SUM(revenue) > 2000
  )
  OR
  -- Condition 4: never purchased with destination Miami
  o.user_id NOT IN (
    SELECT user_id
    FROM orders
    WHERE destination = 'Miami'
  )

WITH race_counts AS (
    SELECT
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM superhero
    GROUP BY gender_id, race_id
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        cnt,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS top_rank,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt ASC) AS bottom_rank
    FROM race_counts
)
SELECT
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    cnt,
    top_rank,
    bottom_rank,
    CASE
        WHEN top_rank <= 5 AND bottom_rank <= 5 THEN 'Both'
        WHEN top_rank <= 5 THEN 'Top 5'
        WHEN bottom_rank <= 5 THEN 'Bottom 5'
    END AS category
FROM ranked
WHERE top_rank <= 5 OR bottom_rank <= 5
ORDER BY gender_id, top_rank

SELECT
    base_cls.relname AS base_table,
    base_att.attname AS base_col,
    ref_cls.relname AS referencing_table,
    ref_att.attname AS referencing_col,
    'CONSTRAINT ' || c.conname || ' ' || pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class base_cls ON base_cls.oid = c.confrelid
JOIN pg_class ref_cls ON ref_cls.oid = c.conrelid
CROSS JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(c.conkey, c.confkey) AS u(conkey_num, confkey_num)
JOIN pg_attribute ref_att ON ref_att.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ref_att.attnum = u.conkey_num
JOIN pg_attribute base_att ON base_att.attrelid = c.confrelid AND base_att.attnum = u.confkey_num
WHERE c.contype = 'f'
  AND c.confrelid = (SELECT oid FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'users')
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
ORDER BY ref_cls.relname, ref_att.attname

WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        m.home_team_goal + m.away_team_goal AS total_goals,
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (ORDER BY m.id, m.date ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS grp
    FROM match m
),
draw_rows AS (
    SELECT id, date, grp
    FROM base
    WHERE is_draw = 1
),
agg AS (
    SELECT
        b.grp,
        SUM(CASE WHEN b.is_draw = 0 THEN b.total_goals ELSE 0 END) AS amount
    FROM base b
    WHERE b.grp > 0
    GROUP BY b.grp
)
SELECT
    d.id,
    d.date,
    1 AS invoiced,
    COALESCE(a.amount, 0) AS amount
FROM draw_rows d
LEFT JOIN agg a ON d.grp = a.grp
ORDER BY d.id, d.date

)

WITH team_stats AS (
    SELECT
        team_api_id,
        COUNT(*) AS matches,
        SUM(CASE WHEN is_win = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS victories,

        SUM(CASE WHEN is_draw = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS draws
    FROM (
        SELECT
            home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
            CASE WHEN home_team_goal > away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_win,
            CASE WHEN home_team_goal < away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_loss,
            CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw
        FROM match
        UNION ALL
        SELECT
            away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
            CASE WHEN away_team_goal > home_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_win,
            CASE WHEN away_team_goal < home_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_loss,
            CASE WHEN away_team_goal = home_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw
        FROM match
    ) all_matches
    GROUP BY team_api_id
)

WITH RECURSIVE Hierarchy AS (
    -- Assign hierarchical levels: top boss is level 0
    SELECT id, bossid, salary, 0 AS hierarchical_level
    FROM staff
    WHERE bossid IS NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT s.id, s.bossid, s.salary, h.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM staff s
    INNER JOIN Hierarchy h ON s.bossid = h.id
),
-- Walk up the management chain for each employee
ChainWalk AS (
    -- Start: each employee with their direct boss (depth 1)
    SELECT 
        e.id AS emp_id,
        e.salary AS emp_salary,
        e.hierarchical_level AS emp_level,
        b.id AS current_boss_id,
        b.salary AS current_boss_salary,
        b.bossid AS next_boss_id,
        1 AS depth
    FROM Hierarchy e
    INNER JOIN Hierarchy b ON e.bossid = b.id
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Go one level up
    SELECT 
        cw.emp_id,
        cw.emp_salary,
        cw.emp_level,
        b.id AS current_boss_id,
        b.salary AS current_boss_salary,
        b.bossid AS next_boss_id,
        cw.depth + 1
    FROM ChainWalk cw
    INNER JOIN Hierarchy b ON cw.next_boss_id = b.id
),
-- Filter to only indirect managers (depth >= 2) meeting salary condition
IndirectManagers AS (
    SELECT 
        emp_id,
        emp_salary,
        emp_level,
        current_boss_id,
        current_boss_salary,
        depth,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY emp_id ORDER BY depth ASC) AS rn
    FROM ChainWalk
    WHERE depth >= 2
      AND current_boss_salary >= 2 * emp_salary
)
SELECT 
    im.emp_id AS employeeId,
    im.emp_salary AS employeeSalary,
    im.emp_level AS hierarchical_level,
    im.current_boss_id AS bossId,
    im.current_boss_salary AS bossSalary
FROM IndirectManagers im
WHERE im.rn = 1
ORDER BY im.emp_id

        -
        (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (CASE 
            WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date 
            THEN begin_date 
            ELSE (date_trunc('month', begin_date) + interval '1 month')::date 
        END)) * 12 
         + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (CASE 
            WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date

            ELSE (date_trunc('month', begin_date) + interval '1 month')::date

    )::int AS full_calendar_months

WITH ordered_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,

        LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id ORDER BY ts) AS prev_ts,

    FROM superhero_activities
),
grouped_activities AS (
    SELECT 
        ts,
        superhero_id,
        activity_code,
        SUM(
            CASE 
                WHEN prev_ts IS NULL 
                     OR activity_code != prev_activity_code 
                     OR ts - prev_ts > INTERVAL '5 minutes'
                THEN 1 
                ELSE 0 
            END
        ) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id ORDER BY ts) AS grp
    FROM ordered_activities
)
SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) AS interval_start,
    MAX(ts) + INTERVAL '5 minutes' AS interval_end,
    COUNT(*) * INTERVAL '5 minutes' AS total_duration
FROM grouped_activities

WITH qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
),
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL AND subgroup2 != 'nan'
),
-- Combine all items with their subgroup labels (prefixed to distinguish)
combined AS (
    SELECT item, 'sg1_' || subgroup AS subgroup_label FROM qr1
    UNION
    SELECT item, 'sg2_' || subgroup AS subgroup_label FROM qr2
),
-- Build edges between subgroup labels that share at least one item
edges AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT a.subgroup_label AS sg_a, b.subgroup_label AS sg_b
    FROM combined a
    JOIN combined b ON a.item = b.item AND a.subgroup_label <= b.subgroup_label
),
-- Find connected components via recursive CTE
-- Start: each subgroup_label maps to the minimum connected label
nodes AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT subgroup_label FROM combined
),
-- Recursive closure to propagate minimum label through edges
closure AS (
    SELECT subgroup_label, subgroup_label AS component FROM nodes
    UNION
    SELECT e.sg_b, c.component
    FROM closure c
    JOIN edges e ON c.subgroup_label = e.sg_a AND c.component < e.sg_b
    UNION
    SELECT e.sg_a, c.component
    FROM closure c
    JOIN edges e ON c.subgroup_label = e.sg_b AND c.component < e.sg_a
),
-- For each subgroup_label, the component is the minimum reachable label
components AS (
    SELECT subgroup_label, MIN(component) AS component
    FROM closure
    GROUP BY subgroup_label
),
-- Assign dense_rank as group id based on component
group_map AS (
    SELECT subgroup_label, DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY component) AS group_id
    FROM components
),
-- Join back to get items with their group
item_groups AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c.item, gm.group_id
    FROM combined c
    JOIN group_map gm ON c.subgroup_label = gm.subgroup_label
),
-- Also get subgroup1 and subgroup2 for display
final AS (
    SELECT
        ig.item,
        qr1.subgroup AS subgroup1,
        qr2.subgroup AS subgroup2,
        ig.group_id
    FROM item_groups ig
    LEFT JOIN qr1 ON ig.item = qr1.item
    LEFT JOIN qr2 ON ig.item = qr2.item
)
SELECT item, subgroup1, subgroup2, group_id AS "group"
FROM final
ORDER BY group_id, item

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text)
RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text) AS $func$
SELECT
    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END,
    c.msg
FROM words_chat c
JOIN words_games g USING (gid)
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2)
JOIN words_users req ON req.uid = s.uid
LEFT JOIN words_users sender ON sender.uid = c.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid
  AND s.social = in_social
  AND s.sid = in_sid
  AND (
      req.muted = true              -- if the requesting user is muted, show all messages
      OR sender.muted = false        -- if the requesting user is not muted, only show messages from non-muted users
      OR sender.uid = s.uid          -- always show the user's own messages
  )
ORDER BY c.created ASC;
$func$ LANGUAGE sql

WITH last_per_station AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT
      *,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid
        ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
      ) AS rn
    FROM transactions_1k
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
),
annotated AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    -- Among amount<10 rows, rank chronologically to find the first
    CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY customerid, (CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END)
        ORDER BY date ASC, time ASC, transactionid ASC
      )
    END AS issue_first_rank,
    -- Among amount>=10 rows, rank reverse-chronologically to find the last
    CASE WHEN amount >= 10 THEN
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY customerid, (CASE WHEN amount >= 10 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END)
        ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
      )
    END AS noissue_last_rank,
    -- Does this customer have any issue?
    COUNT(CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY customerid) AS issue_count
  FROM last_per_station
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  gasstationid,
  amount
FROM annotated
WHERE
  (issue_count > 0 AND issue_first_rank = 1)
  OR (issue_count = 0 AND noissue_last_rank = 1)
ORDER BY customerid

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE team_attributes ta
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date
    FROM (
        SELECT
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id
                    ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE ASC
                ),
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) - 1 AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE ta.id = subquery.id;
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes
    AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
WITH RECURSIVE split AS (
    -- Base case: start with the full attribute_value and remaining amount
    SELECT
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        attribute_value AS remaining,
        CASE WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 50 ELSE attribute_value END AS chunk,
        CASE WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_bifurcated
    FROM hero_attribute

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case: keep splitting the remaining amount after subtracting 50
    SELECT
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        remaining - 50 AS remaining,
        CASE WHEN remaining - 50 > 50 THEN 50 ELSE remaining - 50 END AS chunk,
        1 AS is_bifurcated
    FROM split
    WHERE remaining > 50
)
SELECT
    hero_id,
    attribute_id,
    chunk AS attribute_chunk,
    is_bifurcated
FROM split
WHERE chunk > 0

        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY count DESC, fundingtype) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_rows
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_rounded AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        rn,

    FROM cte_with_rn
)

ORDER BY count DESC, fundingtype

    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE NULL END) - SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE NULL END))::numeric / SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE NULL END), 2) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp::date) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS mtd,
    ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp::date) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE NULL END) - SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp::date) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' AND EXTRACT(day FROM timestamp::date) <= EXTRACT(day FROM '2022-01-27'::date - 1) THEN amount ELSE NULL END))::numeric / SUM(CASE WHEN date_trunc('month', timestamp::date) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - 1) - interval '1 month' AND EXTRACT(day FROM timestamp::date) <= EXTRACT(day FROM '2022-01-27'::date - 1) THEN amount ELSE NULL END), 2) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM transactions

WITH RECURSIVE
bottom_up(id, parent_id, color, lvl) AS (
    -- Base case: leaf nodes (no children)
    SELECT s.id, s.parent_id, s.color, LENGTH(s.id) AS lvl
    FROM superhero2 s
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM superhero2 c WHERE c.parent_id = s.id)

    UNION ALL

    SELECT p.id, p.parent_id,
           CASE
               WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bottom_up bu WHERE bu.parent_id = p.id) = 1
                    THEN (SELECT bu.color FROM bottom_up bu WHERE bu.parent_id = p.id LIMIT 1)
               WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT bu.color) FROM bottom_up bu WHERE bu.parent_id = p.id) = 1
                    THEN (SELECT bu.color FROM bottom_up bu WHERE bu.parent_id = p.id LIMIT 1)
               ELSE 'gray'
           END,
           LENGTH(p.id)
    FROM superhero2 p
    JOIN bottom_up ch ON ch.parent_id = p.id
    WHERE p.color IS NULL
),
final_colors AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (id) id, color
    FROM bottom_up
    ORDER BY id, lvl
)
UPDATE superhero2
SET color = final_colors.color
FROM final_colors
WHERE superhero2.id = final_colors.id

        card_name,

        0 AS depth,

    UNION ALL

        c.uuid,
        c.card_name,

    JOIN nodes n ON n.uuid = c.parent_uuid

    COALESCE(array_agg(ch.uuid) FILTER (WHERE ch.uuid IS NOT NULL), ARRAY[]::uuid[]) AS child_uuids,
    COALESCE(array_agg(ch.card_name) FILTER (WHERE ch.uuid IS NOT NULL), ARRAY[]::text[]) AS child_names
FROM nodes p

GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name, p.depth, p.path

WITH RECURSIVE craft_cost AS (
    -- Base: for each item, gather its direct resource costs
    SELECT 
        s.item_id AS root_item_id,
        s.item_id AS current_item_id,
        1 AS parent_quantity,
        s.quantity,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        s.uses_item_price
    FROM store s

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive: expand uses_item_id into its components
    SELECT 
        cc.root_item_id,
        s.item_id AS current_item_id,
        cc.quantity * cc.parent_quantity,
        s.quantity,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        s.uses_item_price
    FROM craft_cost cc
    JOIN store s ON s.item_id = cc.uses_item_id
    WHERE cc.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    root_item_id AS item_id,
    MAX(st.item_price) AS retail_price,
    (SELECT SUM(COALESCE(s2.resource_price * s2.quantity, 0) + COALESCE(s2.uses_item_price * s2.quantity, 0)) FROM store s2 WHERE s2.item_id = root_item_id) AS partial_craft_price,
    SUM(COALESCE(cc.resource_price * cc.quantity * cc.parent_quantity, 0)) AS full_craft_price
FROM craft_cost cc
JOIN store st ON st.item_id = cc.root_item_id
WHERE cc.uses_item_id IS NULL OR cc.uses_item_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT item_id FROM store)
GROUP BY root_item_id
ORDER BY root_item_id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id text, row_num text)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS
$func$
DECLARE
    cols text[];
    col text;
    update_sql text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns except the id and row_num columns
    SELECT array_agg(column_name::text)
    INTO cols
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_name = tbl
      AND c.is_nullable = 'YES'
      AND c.column_name <> id
      AND c.column_name <> row_num;

    IF cols IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Build a single UPDATE using a CTE that computes forward-filled values
    -- Forward fill: for each nullable column, use the last non-null value
    -- partitioned by id and ordered by row_num
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY cols
    LOOP
        update_sql := format(
            'UPDATE %1$I t SET %2$I = sub.ff_val
             FROM (
                SELECT ctid AS rid,
                       FIRST_VALUE(%2$I) OVER (
                           PARTITION BY %3$I, grp ORDER BY %4$I
                       ) AS ff_val
                FROM (
                    SELECT ctid,
                           %2$I,
                           %3$I,
                           %4$I,
                           COUNT(%2$I) OVER (
                               PARTITION BY %3$I ORDER BY %4$I
                               ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                           ) AS grp
                    FROM %1$I
                ) grouped
             ) sub
             WHERE t.ctid = sub.rid
               AND t.%2$I IS NULL
               AND sub.ff_val IS NOT NULL',
            tbl, col, id, row_num
        );
        EXECUTE update_sql;
    END LOOP;
END
$func$

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::bigint::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN fe.end_date_key IS NULL OR fe.start_date_key = fe.end_date_key 
            THEN NULL
            ELSE to_date(fe.end_date_key::bigint::text, 'YYYYMMDD')
        END AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MIN(start_date) FROM card_activity)),
        date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE),
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_start
)
SELECT 
    m.month_start AS month,
    COALESCE(sa.set_name, 'Unknown') AS set_name,
    COUNT(*) AS existing_cards
FROM months m
INNER JOIN card_activity sa 
    ON sa.start_date < (m.month_start + INTERVAL '1 month')
    AND (sa.end_date IS NULL OR sa.end_date >= m.month_start)
GROUP BY m.month_start, sa.set_name
ORDER BY m.month_start, sa.set_name

WITH RECURSIVE user_pairs AS (
    -- Find direct connections between users (shared ref1 or shared ref2)
    SELECT u1.id AS id1, u2.id AS id2
    FROM users u1
    JOIN users u2 ON u1.id < u2.id
        AND (u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2)
),
-- Build connected components using recursive CTE
components AS (
    -- Each user starts as its own component (represented by min id)
    SELECT id, id AS component FROM users
    UNION
    SELECT u.id, LEAST(c.component, u2.component) AS component
    FROM components c
    JOIN user_pairs p ON c.id = p.id1 OR c.id = p.id2
    JOIN users u ON u.id = CASE WHEN c.id = p.id1 THEN p.id2 ELSE p.id1 END
    JOIN components u2 ON u2.id = u.id
    WHERE LEAST(c.component, u2.component) < GREATEST(c.component, u2.component)
),
final_components AS (
    SELECT id, MIN(component) AS component
    FROM components
    GROUP BY id
),
-- Map each user to its merged group
user_groups AS (
    SELECT u.id, u.username, u.ref1, u.ref2, fc.component
    FROM users u
    JOIN final_components fc ON u.id = fc.id
),
-- Assets matched to users via ref1 or ref2
matched_assets AS (
    SELECT a.id AS asset_id, a.name AS asset_name, a.owner, ug.component
    FROM assets a
    JOIN user_groups ug ON a.owner = ug.ref1 OR a.owner = ug.ref2
),
-- Assets not matched to any user
unmatched_assets AS (
    SELECT a.id AS asset_id, a.name AS asset_name, a.owner
    FROM assets a
    LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
    WHERE u.id IS NULL
),
-- Aggregate matched groups
matched_result AS (
    SELECT
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ug.id ORDER BY ug.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ug.username ORDER BY ug.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ug.ref1 ORDER BY ug.ref1) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ug.ref2 ORDER BY ug.ref2) AS refs2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT ma.asset_id) AS asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ma.asset_id ORDER BY ma.asset_id) AS asset_ids
    FROM user_groups ug
    LEFT JOIN matched_assets ma ON ug.component = ma.component
    GROUP BY ug.component
),
-- Unmatched assets as standalone rows
unmatched_result AS (
    SELECT
        NULL::integer[] AS ids,
        NULL::text[] AS usernames,
        NULL::text[] AS refs1,
        ARRAY[ua.owner] AS refs2,
        COUNT(ua.asset_id) AS asset_count,
        ARRAY_AGG(ua.asset_id ORDER BY ua.asset_id) AS asset_ids
    FROM unmatched_assets ua
    GROUP BY ua.owner
)
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids
FROM matched_result
UNION ALL
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids
FROM unmatched_result
ORDER BY asset_ids
